key_insight: Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potentials can capture screw-dislocation physics in refractory bcc metals much more accurately than prior classical and machine-learning models, especially for Mo and W.
engineering_relevance: Improved dislocation and Peierls-barrier prediction enables more reliable mesoscale-to-atomistic design of high-temperature, high-strength refractory alloys.
method: Extended an existing DFT database for group 5 and 6 refractory metals (V, Nb, Ta, Mo, W).
Trained element-specific ACE interatomic potentials to model screw-dislocation glide and core energetics.
Evaluated Peierls barriers, glide planes, and kink-pair activation enthalpies against DFT and continuum models.
evidence_signal: The ACE models achieve near-DFT agreement for Mo and W, with substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, and they reproduce kink-pair activation enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient proof of transferability; performance depends strongly on database coverage and element-specific energetics.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
